_policy: Every consciously deferred piece of work gets a row: what was deferred, why, what enforcement exists meanwhile, and a review-by date. `cargo xtask deferrals --check` (weekly scheduled CI) fails once a row passes its date un-re-decided — a deferral nobody re-examines is a silent forever. Re-deciding means either building the thing (delete the row) or re-dating it with a fresh reason in the same commit. Decisions intended to be permanent do not belong here; they get ADRs.

deferrals.what: The pinned suite's fourteen auth/* client scenarios (OAuth metadata discovery, registration, PKCE flows) as host-SUT gates
deferrals.why: Requires a full OAuth client; ADR-0009 §5 scoped it out of M3 exactly as the registry's TRAN-009 exclusion records for the server side
deferrals.meanwhile: TRAN-009 documented exclusion; the four protocol scenarios gate in CI

deferrals.what: Re-decided 2026-06-27 — do NOT file the 'add POST-path resumption' fix. The 2026-07-28 draft removes SSE stream resumability and Last-Event-ID from Streamable HTTP (register 1.5d Major #9, SEP-2575), so that fix is obsoleted by the spec. Re-scoped to the durable residue: assess whether rmcp's hang on early POST-stream close (instead of surfacing a re-issuable error, which the new text REQUIRES) is worth filing — judged against the FINAL spec text plus the draft suite's transport/sse scenarios
deferrals.why: The original premise (2025-11-25 requires the Last-Event-ID resumption rmcp lacks on the POST path) is being deleted by 2026-07-28; filing the add-resumption fix now would ask upstream to build a mechanism the next revision removes, while upstream is already implementing the removal (register 3.11). The re-scoped hang question cannot be verified until the final text ships (2026-07-28) and the draft suite scenarios stabilize
deferrals.meanwhile: Register rows 3.12 + 1.5d carry the reconciliation; the dossier (docs/reports/rmcp-sse-resumption-dossier.md) records that the spec removes the requirement and re-scopes to the hang. The 2025-11-25 mechanism remains exact — re-verified first-hand 2026-06-27 at rmcp head eb435c6 (raw_sse_to_jsonrpc l.303 'without reconnection logic'; POST arms l.778-779/803-804; SseAutoReconnectStream GET-only at l.584/720) — and the reference host still passes sse-retry 3/3, so the evidence is preserved if the decision reopens

deferrals.what: Re-check rust-sdk#902 (everything-server offer): respond, re-ping, or record the outcome when the R9 60-day clock runs out
deferrals.why: The offer was posted 2026-06-11; the risk register's R9 gives upstream 60 days before the offer is re-evaluated
deferrals.meanwhile: Risk R9 row; issue #9 tracks the conversation

deferrals.what: Re-verify every register row dated 2026-06-09..12 in docs/plan/01-ecosystem-context.md before external use
deferrals.why: The register's own maintenance rule 1: rows older than 90 days must be re-verified before citation
deferrals.meanwhile: Rows carry verification dates; CI does not consume row facts

deferrals.what: Bump the suite pin when the 0.2.0 line stabilizes (alpha.3 published 2026-06-12; register rows 2.4/2.16), refresh both expected-failures baselines and the agreement divergence ledger in the same change
deferrals.why: Pin bumps are deliberate PRs with a scenario diff review (03-conformance-strategy §Official-suite version policy)
deferrals.meanwhile: Weekly suite-alpha job runs the alpha dist-tag non-blocking; conformance#338's fix arrives with the bump and its baseline entry will go stale loudly

deferrals.what: Adopt a released rmcp carrying the enumNames fix (rust-sdk#905, merged to main 2026-06-20) once it ships (v1.8.0 planning, rust-sdk#850); in the same dependency-bump PR update register 3.8/3.10 and drop the tests/roundtrip.rs pin + the interactive.rs workaround note
deferrals.why: The fix is merged upstream but not yet on crates.io (latest published rmcp is 1.7.0, which still drops enumNames); SDK/dependency bumps are deliberate, released-version-only changes per the engineering standards (floors are versions we actually test against)
deferrals.meanwhile: Pinned rmcp 1.7.0 still drops the field; tests/roundtrip.rs pins that current behavior and fails LOUDLY the moment a bumped rmcp preserves enumNames, forcing this update; register 3.8 records the upstream resolution
